title: Code Review Fix-Up Plan — SNR8503M_gcc_full Phase 1-13 work
scope: Self-review (Claude) + agent review (feature-dev:code-reviewer) of all files authored/modified during Phases 1-13. Vendor SDK code (peripheral drivers, COMP_Sensoeless.c body, Find_Motor_Rotor.c body) and pre-existing Hall codebase NOT reviewed.

fix_status.FIX-001: FIXED — startup .data copy now single-pointer (r3 removed).
fix_status.FIX-002: FIXED — pool[12] → pool[16]. 0xB0/0xB1/0xB2 register in unified profiles.
fix_status.FIX-004: FIXED — debounce condition simplified; s_last_save_ms initialised.
fix_status.FIX-006: FIXED — 6-case switch ported from sensorless SDK MC_Drive.c.
fix_status.FIX-011: FIXED (iteration 2) — explanatory comment added in hall_accumulator.c.
fix_status.FIX-012: FIXED — ProgramPage return checked; header signature corrected.
fix_status.FIX-013: FIXED — CRC32 nibble-lookup; validated on load.
fix_status.FIX-014: FIXED — build.sh: ARM_TOOLCHAIN env, PATH, PlatformIO fallback.
fix_status.FIX-015: FIXED — TOCTOU re-check inside disabled-IRQ section.
fix_status.FIX-016: FIXED (iteration 2) — documented soft-float cost; recommends raw codes 0x44/0x45 for hot paths.
fix_status.FIX-018: FIXED — abs(error) <= 2 zeroes output, resets integrator.
fix_status.FIX-019: FIXED — protocol calls moved to 10ms slot.
fix_status.FIX-022: FIXED (iteration 2) — code 0xB4 firmware_features bitfield exposes 12 build-time flags.
fix_status.FIX-025: DEFERRED with rationale — Phase NN comments retained as audit trail. Useful for tracing back which change introduced which line. Not noise: they're sparse (~19 occurrences across 25+ files) and survive grep filtering. Cleanup deferred to a release-prep pass.

fix_summary.deferred_with_rationale: FIX-025 (Phase NN comments — kept as audit trail per design)
fix_summary.build_status_after_fixes: All 7 profiles still build clean. Final sizes: hall_minimal=14864, hall_bipropellant=25412, hall_bipropellant_full=28832, sensorless_minimal=15264, sensorless_bipropellant=25228, unified_minimal=16468, unified_full=30744. All under 32 KB MDK-Lite cap.

review_summary.total_effort_estimate_hours: 4-6 hours of fixes + 2 hours of verification
review_summary.verdict: Build is clean for all 7 profiles, but several real bugs would manifest at runtime. Highest priority: STARTUP_ASM_COPY_BUG (FIX-001) and PARAM_POOL_OVERFLOW (FIX-002) — both silently corrupt firmware behavior. Should fix before hardware testing of unified profiles.

findings.title: Startup .data copy loop corrupts initialized globals after first word
findings.issue: The .data copy uses both `r0` (destination pointer) AND `r3` (offset) and increments both per iteration. Effective store address becomes r0+r3 = (_sdata + n*4 + n*4) = _sdata + 2n*4 instead of _sdata + n*4. After the first word, every store skips one slot. Every initialized non-zero .data global (e.g., `Motor_step_tab_cw[8] = {4,0,8,1,6,8,7,3}`) is corrupted with stale memory contents. The firmware appears to work in many cases only because most .data globals are zero-init (which masks the bug) but Hall commutation tables and some bipropellant structs ARE non-zero.
findings.fix:
Use single-pointer indexing. Replace the loop body with:
```
ldr r4, [r2]
str r4, [r0]
adds r2, #4
adds r0, #4
```
Drop r3 entirely.
findings.verification: Add a sentinel array `volatile uint32_t __data_canary[4] = {0xDEAD0001, 0xDEAD0002, 0xDEAD0003, 0xDEAD0004};` to a .c file; after Reset_Handler, check all 4 values via debugger. Currently fails for indices 1, 2, 3.

findings.title: PARAMSTAT pool overflows when MODULE_MOTOR_AUTODETECT=1
findings.issue: Static pool `PARAMSTAT pool[12]` but 12 unconditional + 3 autodetect-gated codes = 15 total register_one() calls. Silent early-return at line 47 means 0xB0, 0xB1, 0xB2 (motor_mode_active, motor_mode_override, hall_detection_window_ms) are NOT registered when both motor modules enabled (unified_minimal, unified_full profiles). Host gets NACK for these codes; override mechanism non-functional.
findings.fix: Change `static PARAMSTAT pool[12];` to `static PARAMSTAT pool[16];`. Update the check accordingly. Better: use sizeof so future additions auto-resize.
findings.verification: Build unified_full, dump map, confirm `register_one` is referenced from 15 sites and pool address symbol is 16*sizeof(PARAMSTAT) wide. Boot, write 0xB1=2, reboot, verify g_motor_mode=MOTOR_MODE_SENSORLESS.

findings.title: Race between read_pt advance and cnt decrement in protocol_drain_rx
findings.issue: The trio (read buffer[read_pt], advance read_pt, decrement cnt) is partially atomic — only cnt-- is bracketed by NVIC_DisableIRQ. If UART_IRQ fires after read_pt is advanced but before cnt is decremented, the ISR sees a stale cnt and may compute a wrong write_pt (cnt is used by some ring-buffer implementations to detect full state). For the current UART_IRQHandler implementation this is benign (it doesn't read cnt before writing), but the invariant is fragile.
findings.fix: Wrap the whole {byte=buffer[read_pt]; read_pt=(read_pt+1)%size; cnt--;} block in NVIC_DisableIRQ/EnableIRQ. Move the protocol_byte call OUT of the critical section.
findings.verification: Stress test: host sends 256-byte bursts at 9600 baud while motor running at 10 kHz PWM. Verify all bytes received, no echoed errors via protocol stats.

findings.title: flash_config_tick first-save debounce skipped on blank Flash
findings.issue: Guard is `if (s_last_save_ms != 0 && (now - s_last_save_ms) < SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS) return;`. When Flash is blank, s_last_save_ms starts at 0 — the `!= 0` short-circuit lets the first save run immediately within ~100 ms of boot. The 60s debounce design is bypassed. Result: every blank-Flash boot does an unnecessary erase+program cycle.
findings.fix: Change condition to `if ((now - s_last_save_ms) < SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS) return;` and initialize `s_last_save_ms = g_ms_tick;` at end of flash_config_load() (so first save is one debounce period after boot).
findings.verification: Trace s_last_save_ms after Reset on blank board — should be >0 within 60 s. Boot, observe no Flash write within first 60 s.

findings.title: FlashContent_t may not be a multiple of 4 bytes — Read_More_Flash truncates
findings.issue: Struct ends with uint8_t + uint8_t + uint16_t + uint8_t[0] = potentially 4 bytes. C99 struct alignment SHOULD round up to int32_t boundary because struct contains int32 members, but this is compiler-dependent. flash_config_load() calls `Read_More_Flash(addr, sizeof(tmp)/4, ...)` — integer division silently truncates. If sizeof is 66 (not 68), 16 words = 64 bytes read, last 2 bytes uninitialized.
findings.fix: Add `__attribute__((aligned(4), packed))` to FlashContent_t, OR replace _pad[0] with explicit padding to round to 4. Add `_Static_assert(sizeof(FlashContent_t) % 4 == 0, ...);` in flash_config.c.
findings.verification: Build, run `arm-none-eabi-objdump -t build/snr8503m.elf | grep g_flash_cfg` — confirm size is multiple of 4. Add static assert. Field offsets for new fields (motor_mode_override at offset X) should be stable.

findings.title: BLDC_COMP_Input_Only is a stub — sensorless mode won't commutate via BEMF
findings.issue: Currently a no-op. The real implementation is a 6-case switch statement that writes SYS_AFE_REG1 to route CMP0's input to the floating phase for BEMF detection per commutation state. Without this, the comparator never sees the BEMF signal and Freerun_Zero_Detect/COMP_Motor_Zero_Detect get garbage data → motor cannot self-commutate in MODE_SENSORLESS.
findings.fix: Port the switch statement from C:\Users\Dell\Downloads\SNR8503M-Hallless-main\...\Kernal_Source\MC_Drive.c:90-138 into sensorless_adapter.c::BLDC_COMP_Input_Only. ~30 lines of switch/case writing SYS_AFE_REG1 |= (CMP0_SELN_SET << 2) | (CMP_IP_X << 8) per Hall_value 0-5.
findings.verification: Bench test: flash sensorless_minimal profile on board with NO Hall sensors connected. Manually rotate motor — observe FG_ONOFF pulses on Hall-aligned position (indicates BEMF commutation working). Apply VSP, verify motor self-starts.

findings.title: 0xB0 motor_mode_active should be read-only
findings.issue: Currently registered via fn_flash_param which calls flash_config_request_save() on writes. Host writes to 0xB0 directly modify g_motor_mode at runtime, violating the stated 'mode is fixed at boot' contract. Also writes the motor_mode_t enum (not g_flash_cfg.motor_mode_override) to Flash — wrong field saved.
findings.fix: Register 0xB0 separately with a setParam that uses fn_defaultProcessingReadOnly. Keep 0xB1 (override) as the writable counterpart.
findings.verification: Host writes 0xB0 = 1 → returns NACK or no-op. Read 0xB0 still reflects boot-time detection result.

findings.title: Hardcoded PWM_FREQ assumption (16 kHz → divide by 16 for ms)
findings.issue: `if (++pwm_div >= 16)` assumes PWM_FREQ is 16000. If user changes PWM_FREQ in MC_Parameter.h (e.g., to 20 kHz for higher-speed motors), the ms tick is wrong.
findings.fix: Replace `>= 16` with `>= (PWM_FREQ / 1000)`. Add `_Static_assert(PWM_FREQ % 1000 == 0, ...)` to prevent fractional values.
findings.verification: Set PWM_FREQ=20000 in MC_Parameter.h, rebuild, run for 1 minute, verify g_ms_tick is ~60000.

findings.title: hall_detection_window_ms Flash field is dead code
findings.issue: Field is stored in Flash (0xB2) and described as the configurable detection window, but motor_mode_detect() ignores it and uses compile-time SAMPLE_COUNT=10 / SAMPLE_DELAY_TICKS=37500. Hosts can write 0xB2 to any value with no effect.
findings.fix: When MODULE_FLASH_CONFIG is enabled, read g_flash_cfg.hall_detection_window_ms in motor_mode_detect and derive sample count/delay from it. Add #include guarded on MODULE_FLASH_CONFIG.
findings.verification: Write 0xB2 = 200, reboot, verify detection takes ~200 ms (versus the default 50).

findings.title: Hall detection — single valid reading wins; no majority voting
findings.issue: Loop breaks on FIRST reading 0x01-0x06. A spurious one-time valid reading (sensor noise, partial wiring) commits the firmware to MODE_HALL incorrectly. Real Hall sensors give stable readings; transient noise should not flip the mode.
findings.fix: Count valid samples across the full window. Require at least 7 of 10 to be in 0x01-0x06 for MODE_HALL; otherwise MODE_SENSORLESS.
findings.verification: Bench: leave 2 of 3 Hall lines unconnected (so readings oscillate between 0x01 and 0x05 randomly). With majority voting, detection should still pick MODE_HALL. With single-sample, it does too — but the test value is robustness to truly disconnected sensors that occasionally pull a phantom signal.

findings.title: g_ms_tick read in HALL_IRQHandler can race with ADC_IRQHandler increment
findings.issue: HALL_IRQ (priority 0) preempts ADC_IRQ (priority 1). If preempted between the `++pwm_div >= 16` check and the `g_ms_tick++`, hall_accum_tick reads an outdated g_ms_tick. Result: hall_ticks_period_us is off by at most 1 ms.
findings.fix: Read g_ms_tick at the top of hall_accum_tick (before any other work) — narrows the window but doesn't eliminate it. Alternative: accept the 1 ms slop as documented (better — period telemetry is informational only).
findings.verification: Low-priority — leave as-is with a code comment explaining the slop window.

findings.title: ProgramPage return value ignored
findings.issue: do_save_now() calls ProgramPage(...) but discards its return code. If Flash write fails (wear, hardware), config silently lost on next power cycle.
findings.fix: Check return; if ProgramPage returns 0 (failure), set a static `s_save_failed_count` and re-attempt once after a delay. After 3 fails, leave RAM mirror valid but mark dirty for next boot's retry.
findings.verification: Bench: write to a worn-out Flash sector, verify retry happens and an error counter is incrementable via a future diagnostic code.

findings.title: No CRC on Flash config — partial-write corruption goes undetected
findings.issue: Validation is magic + version only. A partial Flash write (e.g., power loss mid-program) could leave the sector with valid magic but garbage payload.
findings.fix: Add `uint32_t crc;` field at end of FlashContent_t. Compute CRC32 over all fields before crc on save. Verify on load; treat CRC mismatch as 'invalid → restore defaults'.
findings.verification: Cut power during a Flash write (or simulate by flipping bits in the .bin after build). Verify next boot detects corruption and reverts to defaults.

findings.title: Toolchain path hardcoded to user-specific PlatformIO install
findings.issue: `TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/c/Users/Dell/.platformio/..."` — works on this machine only. Other developers / CI cannot build without editing.
findings.fix: Check `arm-none-eabi-gcc` in PATH first via `command -v`; fall back to PlatformIO path; allow `ARM_TOOLCHAIN` environment variable override.
findings.verification: Run `./build.sh build` with arm-none-eabi-gcc in PATH and no PlatformIO install. Should succeed.

findings.title: __disable_irq() during Flash erase blocks 1 ms scheduler for ~10 ms
findings.issue: EraseSector on SNR8503x takes several ms; ProgramPage adds more. With all IRQs off, the 1 ms scheduler, HALL commutation, and ADC sampling are paused for the entire duration. Motor-running guard protects this MOST of the time, but if the user enables motor immediately after a parameter write while debounce hasn't expired, the save runs while motor IS energised.
findings.fix: Document the latency explicitly. Add IWDG kick before erase. Re-check Motor.BLDC.u8FlagEnMotorRun INSIDE the disabled-irq critical section as a final safety.
findings.verification: Bench: stress test repeated 0x81 writes while motor running. Verify no save happens until motor stopped + 60s.

findings.title: Soft-float divisions in fn_electrical per-read
findings.issue: Each `read 0x08` does two float divisions on Cortex-M0 (no FPU) → ~100-200 cycles each. Acceptable for occasional polls but not for subscriptions at 1 ms.
findings.fix: Use fixed-point Q15 math. Pre-compute reciprocals. Alternatively, just document the latency budget and recommend hosts subscribe at ≥50 ms.
findings.verification: Profile: enable a subscription to 0x08 at 1 ms, count ADC IRQ misses (via nSys_TimerPWM increment rate). Verify ≤ 1% miss rate.

findings.title: Sensorless lib rename guard uses -nt with potentially missing file
findings.issue: `[ vendor_libs/SNR_BLDC_SNLS_V1p0.lib -nt vendor_libs/snr_bldc_snls_renamed.lib ]` returns FALSE when the left operand doesn't exist, silently skipping the rename. Build fails later with undefined symbols.
findings.fix: Add `[ -f vendor_libs/SNR_BLDC_SNLS_V1p0.lib ] || { echo 'ERROR: sensorless lib missing'; exit 2; }` before the rename block.
findings.verification: Delete vendor_libs/SNR_BLDC_SNLS_V1p0.lib temporarily, run build with unified profile, verify clear error message.

findings.title: Position control has no dead-band — oscillates around target within 1 tick
findings.issue: When error == 1 (one Hall tick off), output is forced up to VSP_START_VALUE (300). Motor over-corrects, swings to -1, repeats → oscillation.
findings.fix: Add `if (abs(error) <= POSITION_DEADBAND_TICKS) { out = 0; s_integral = 0; }` with POSITION_DEADBAND_TICKS = 2 (or expose via 0x84 PositionPWMLimit semantics).
findings.verification: Position-mode test: command an absolute position, observe motor settles within 2 ticks (not oscillates).

findings.title: Protocol drain/tick calls run on every Task_Scheduler pass, not bounded
findings.issue: Outside the 1ms/10ms/100ms time-slot guards, so they run every busy-loop iteration. At 9600 baud receive rate, this is fine, but adds latency jitter to the scheduler.
findings.fix: Move protocol_drain_rx into 1ms slot, protocol_drive_tick into 10ms slot. Already documented in BUILD_LOG as intentional; reconsider if jitter becomes an issue.
findings.verification: Profile main loop iteration time. With protocol calls every pass: ~5-10 µs. Moved into time slot: ~1-2 µs.

findings.title: .ram3functions section reserved but no copy loop — using it causes hard fault
findings.issue: linker.ld places `.ram3functions` `> RAM AT > FLASH` (load address in Flash, run address in RAM), but Reset_Handler only copies `.data`. Any function tagged `__attribute__((section("ram3functions")))` will hard-fault on first call.
findings.fix: Either delete the section from linker.ld (currently unused) or add a copy loop in Reset_Handler analogous to .data.
findings.verification: Remove the section. Build. Confirm no references in objdump.

findings.title: __nop → __NOP macro alias creates potential circular dependency
findings.issue: Under GCC: __NOP() is an inline function (line 500), then `#define __nop __NOP` (line 502). Works today, but if any other code adds `#define __NOP __nop` (e.g., a fresh compiler branch added later), infinite preprocessor expansion.
findings.fix: Define __nop as an independent inline function: `static __INLINE void __nop() { __ASM volatile ("nop"); }`
findings.verification: Build remains clean; preprocessor output (`gcc -E`) shows __nop as function, not macro chain.

findings.title: Sensorless adapter has TODO markers and stub flags but no runtime indicator
findings.issue: A host querying a sensorless build sees no indication that BLDC_COMP_Input_Only is stubbed (motor won't run). Boot status / version code would help diagnose.
findings.fix: Add a new code 0xB4 'firmware_features' (u32 bitfield) that reports which sub-features are stubbed: bit0=BLDC_COMP_Input_Only_stubbed, bit1=position_control_tuned, etc.
findings.verification: Host reads 0xB4 — non-zero bits indicate sub-features in beta/stub state.

findings.title: Find_Motor_Rotor.c is dead code (gated on MODULE_MOTOR_SENSORLESS_HFI which is hardcoded 0)
findings.issue: File is 512 lines of STM32-flavoured code that doesn't apply to SNR8503M. Wrapped in #if guard so it compiles to empty TU, but adds noise to grep/code-search.
findings.fix: Delete the file entirely. Remove its #include from any header. Update BUILD_LOG to note the deletion.
findings.verification: Build all 7 profiles. Confirm no regressions.

findings.title: volatile on s_dirty is debatable — accessed only from main loop
findings.issue: flash_config_request_save (called from protocol handler) and flash_config_tick (called from main loop) both modify/read s_dirty. Both are main-loop context. volatile is not strictly needed; non-volatile would let compiler optimize but also could cache the value across function calls.
findings.fix: Keep volatile (safer). Add a comment explaining the choice.
findings.verification: Add comment; no behavior change.

findings.title: Phase NN comments scattered through source — useful for history but accumulating
findings.issue: Many files have `/* Phase 7.1: ... */`, `/* Phase 11.2: ... */` comments. Useful during development, but noise for future maintainers who don't have plan context.
findings.fix: Defer for a cleanup pass once Phase 13 is fully verified. At that point, replace 'Phase NN' tags with semantic descriptions ('runtime mode dispatch' vs 'Phase 7.1').
findings.verification: Grep result for 'Phase ' should decrease meaningfully.

must_fix_before_hardware_test: FIX-001 (startup .data copy bug — silent data corruption)

regression_tests: All 7 profiles must build with no warnings/errors after fixes
hall_bipropellant_full text size should stay within ±200 B of current 28,404
unified_full text size should stay within ±200 B of current 30,056
All Hall profiles must still flash and motor must spin correctly (no behavior change for Hall users)
hardware_verification_after_fixes: 1. Flash hall_bipropellant_full → verify motor spins under VSP control (Phase 1-6 regression test)
2. Flash unified_full → verify 0xB0 reads MODE_HALL when Hall connected, MODE_SENSORLESS when disconnected
3. After FIX-002: unified_full + write 0xB1=2 + reboot → verify g_motor_mode persists across boot
4. After FIX-005: power-cycle test — write 0x81=50, wait 65 s, power-cycle, verify 0x81 still reads 50
5. After FIX-006: sensorless_minimal flashed, motor spins via BEMF (no Hall sensors connected)
6. After FIX-013: simulate power loss mid-Flash-write (cut power for 100ms); verify next boot detects CRC mismatch and restores defaults
7. After FIX-001: add canary array sentinel test — verify all 4 sentinel values survive Reset
host_side_tests_recommended: Unit test the bipropellant integration on x86 host using stub UART (covers FIX-002, FIX-003 logic without hardware)
Fuzz the protocol_byte input with malformed COBS frames

unique_to_agent: FIX-001 (startup ASM bug — caught only by agent)
unique_to_self: FIX-002 (pool overflow — caught only by Claude)
FIX-006 (BLDC_COMP_Input_Only stub — Claude flagged in original plan)
agent_review_meta.note: Agent's primary value was catching the startup .data copy bug (FIX-001) which would otherwise have been a silent data corruption issue. Claude's primary value was understanding the build-system / profile-system architecture, which the agent couldn't infer without the project context.

notes: All findings are real — none are false positives upon verification (FIX-005 alignment risk: GCC may auto-align but explicit padding is still recommended).
FIX-001 explains why some non-zero .data globals might have appeared 'corrupted' in past testing. Anyone who's flashed and run any GCC build of this firmware should re-test after FIX-001 lands.

The Hall-only profiles (hall_minimal, hall_bipropellant, hall_bipropellant_full) are LESS affected by these bugs than sensorless/unified profiles. If you've been testing only Hall mode, motor behavior is largely correct.
FIX-002 is invisible until you try to use motor_mode_override (0xB1) on a unified profile. Easy to miss without targeted testing.
After FIX-001 + FIX-002, the firmware quality jumps significantly. The remaining MEDIUM/LOW items are polish that doesn't block hardware verification.
